title: Viorna × pseudococcinea

The plant has been traditionally used for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may help alleviate various health conditions such as wounds, fever, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Viorna include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Viorna include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used in traditional medicine to promote overall well-being and support the immune system.
